# Instructions

The canonical rule files. Both Copilot and Claude read from here — Copilot directly, Claude through synced copies in `.claude/rules/`. Edit only the files in this folder; the mirror is regenerated by `../scripts/sync-claude-rules.py` on every commit.

## Frontmatter

Every file declares its scope with `applyTo: "<glob>"`. The sync script transforms this into `paths: ["<glob>"]` when writing the Claude mirror. A file with no frontmatter, or an empty body, or more than 4,000 characters, will be rejected by the validation workflow.

## What's here

Universal rules apply to all files: `code-standards`, `pr-review`, `patterns`, `user-interface`, `user-experience`. Scoped rules attach to specific directories: `adr-template` and `adr-pr-review` to `docs/adr/**`, `br-review` to `docs/business-rules/**`. Stack-specific code standards live alongside the universal one (e.g. `csharp-code-standards.instructions.md`) and extend rather than replace it.

## When to add a file

Add a new instruction file when a rule applies to a directory or file type that none of the existing files cover. Keep agnostic content out of stack-specific files and stack content out of agnostic ones. If the rule is a one-off architectural decision, write an ADR instead.
